request.system: You are an algorithmic trading strategy researcher...
request.user: Generate 4 trading strategy hypotheses for Betfair Exchange.

message.content: {"hypotheses": [{"name": "bollinger_reversion", "rationale": "Prices on liquid Betfair markets tend to revert to a rolling mean after transient order-flow imbalances. A Bollinger-band signal captures this mean-reversion regime without requiring external data.", "signal_formula": "z = (mid - mean(mid, window)) / std(mid, window); signal = -z when abs(z) > threshold", "params": {"window": {"kind": "int", "low": 10, "high": 100, "default": 20}, "threshold": {"kind": "float", "low": 1.0, "high": 3.0, "default": 2.0}}, "entry_rules": "Enter when z-score exceeds threshold in either direction; bet against the direction of the deviation.", "exit_rules": "Exit when z-score reverts to within 0.5 standard deviations of the mean.", "expected_edge": "Short-term mean-reversion in illiquid ticks driven by noise traders."}, {"name": "microprice_drift", "rationale": "The microprice (volume-weighted mid) leads the true mid on Betfair when book imbalance is persistent. A short lookback drift signal on microprice should capture directional momentum.", "signal_formula": "microprice = (best_ask * best_bid_depth + best_bid * best_ask_depth) / (best_bid_depth + best_ask_depth); drift = microprice - mean(microprice, window)", "params": {"window": {"kind": "int", "low": 5, "high": 50, "default": 10}, "drift_threshold": {"kind": "float", "low": 0.0001, "high": 0.01, "default": 0.001}}, "entry_rules": "Enter in the direction of drift when abs(drift) > drift_threshold.", "exit_rules": "Exit after hold_ticks ticks or when drift reverses sign.", "expected_edge": "Informed order-flow imbalance creates short-lived directional momentum."}, {"name": "spread_capture", "rationale": "When the bid-ask spread is abnormally wide relative to recent history, passive limit orders at the best quotes earn the spread as the market reverts.", "signal_formula": "relative_spread = (best_ask - best_bid) / mid; signal = 1 when relative_spread > mean(relative_spread, window) + k * std(relative_spread, window)", "params": {"window": {"kind": "int", "low": 10, "high": 200, "default": 50}, "k": {"kind": "float", "low": 0.5, "high": 3.0, "default": 1.5}}, "entry_rules": "Post passive orders on both sides of the book when relative spread is wide.", "exit_rules": "Cancel unfilled orders when spread normalises.", "expected_edge": "Wide spreads attract passive market-making; earn spread as market normalises."}, {"name": "book_imbalance_fade", "rationale": "Extreme book imbalance (one side overwhelms the other) often precedes a short-term price reversal as the imbalance resolves.", "signal_formula": "imbalance = (best_bid_depth - best_ask_depth) / (best_bid_depth + best_ask_depth); fade when abs(imbalance) > threshold", "params": {"threshold": {"kind": "float", "low": 0.3, "high": 0.9, "default": 0.7}, "hold_ticks": {"kind": "int", "low": 1, "high": 20, "default": 5}}, "entry_rules": "Fade the imbalance direction when abs(imbalance) exceeds threshold.", "exit_rules": "Exit after hold_ticks or when imbalance drops below 0.2.", "expected_edge": "Order-book imbalance at extreme values has short-lived predictive power."}]}
